prose:
Method-provenance block
Property: Most electrophilic Fukui function value f+ (dimensionless) at the reactive site (atom 1, N) of pyridine.
Level of theory: DFT tier `standard`, i.e. B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI density fitting (PySCF backend via chemkit).
Geometry provenance: single-point charges calculated on the fixed geometry supplied by the user (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz); no further optimisation was performed (DFT//given XYZ).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation model).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet). The N+1 and N–1 states were treated as doublets (charge +1, mult 2; charge –1, mult 2) to obtain f+ and f–, respectively.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill calling PySCF (version as bundled with chemkit, B3LYP functional, def2-TZVP basis, RI enabled).
Convergence & caveats: Single-point SCF calculations for N, N+1 and N–1 states all converged (integrity.status = "ok"). The integrity checker returned trustworthy = true. Warnings reported by the engine are reproduced verbatim below.

Warnings (VERBATIM):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• schema_header present

Reproducibility and files
CLI invocation that generated the result (from result JSON):
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpja0a_you/live.json

The result JSON is at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpja0a_you/live.json and a graphical map of the Fukui indices was written to /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/argo_o3/20260703-000606_pyridine_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png. The engine streamed its full PySCF output to a live .out log in the working directory during the run (path shown at runtime; available for inspection upon request).

Headline result
The most electrophilic site in pyridine is the nitrogen atom (atom 1), with an f+ value of 0.48422572 (dimensionless).
